# Depth-Four Map

<!-- GENERATED FROM docs/editorial/depth-four-plan.json. Do not edit this file directly. Run `npm run generate:depth-four-map` after changing the planning JSON. -->

This is a generated planning map for eventual depth-four coverage. It does not add public nodes, public edges, public prose or sources.

The source of truth is [depth-four-plan.json](depth-four-plan.json). The generated map calculates Coverage depth from navigable planning edges, so manual table counts cannot drift from the planned graph.

## Scope And Guardrails

This planning system separates canonical nodes, navigable edges, contextual strand placements, approved shared candidates, computed shared-node metrics, preview-only Also touches relationships, historical spines and implementation batches.

The atlas remains a contextual knowledge graph, not a strict tree and not a proportional timeline. Time provides the backbone; causality and explanation provide the branches.

## Planning Approval State

Planning status: `draft-not-approved-for-live-implementation`.

The global depth-four plan is a draft inventory. Provisional relationships may remain in the inventory, but an implementation batch is not ready until its relevant nodes, navigable edges, contextual placements and approved shared contexts are explicitly reviewed.

## Table Conventions

- Existing node: already present in `content/nodes.json`; do not duplicate it.
- Candidate: proposed future canonical node; requires a bubble proposal before implementation.
- Coverage depth: calculated shortest route from `bitcoin` through navigable planning edges.
- Historical-spine membership: `Yes` means the node belongs on the approved backbone for that strand; `Branch` means it explains or attaches to the spine.
- Shared: `Approved shared candidate` means the editorial plan intends to reuse the canonical node in more than one context. `Also touches only` means preview-only.
- Review status: `provisional` means not yet semantically approved; `reviewed-for-batch` and `approved-for-implementation` are the only states that satisfy a batch gate.
- Time confidence: High, Medium, Low, Contested or Not applicable.

## Historical Spines

Every main strand requires one approved historical spine before public nodes are added. The spine is a backbone, not the whole graph. Mechanisms and examples branch from it, and the spine must not imply simple replacement or universal progression.

### Money Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bitcoin | provisional | Persistent | Bitcoin and contemporary digital era | High | The root from which the atlas works backwards. | Bitcoin as Money; Settlement; Custody | Do not frame earlier money as inevitably leading to Bitcoin. |
| Digital Money | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | Medium | Places Bitcoin among earlier electronic money attempts without collapsing them together. | Digital Cash; Digital Payments | Do not treat all digital money as decentralised. |
| Modern Fiat and Credit Systems | provisional | Period | Bitcoin and contemporary digital era | High | Names the monetary environment Bitcoin enters and critiques. | Central Banks; Banking Rails; Legal Tender | Avoid anti-fiat slogans and one-country assumptions. |
| Central Banks | provisional | Period | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Central banking concentrates monetary policy, settlement and liquidity functions. | Lender of Last Resort; Monetary Policy | Do not treat every central bank mandate as identical. |
| Paper Claims | provisional | Period | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Transferable claims help bridge custody, banking and later credit money. | Banking; Custody | Do not imply paper claims are the whole story of fiat money. |
| Coinage | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Coinage gives a historical route into marked monetary objects and state authority. | State Coinage; Mints | Do not present coinage as universal or first. |
| Monetary Metals | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Contested | Metals anchor many monetary histories without exhausting them. | Gold; Commodity Money | Gold must not be treated as containing all commodity money. |
| Social Accounting | provisional | Recurring | Deep human history | Contested | Obligations, memory and exchange predate formal money objects. | Social Memory; Tally Sticks; Ledgers | Do not present barter as a universal first stage. |

### Energy Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bitcoin Mining | provisional | Persistent | Bitcoin and contemporary digital era | High | The direct energy-facing Bitcoin practice. | Proof of Work; Difficulty Adjustment; Hashrate; Mining Pools | Do not make mining the whole energy strand. |
| Electrical Grids and Computation | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | High | Mining depends on electrified computation and grid infrastructure. | Energy Markets; Mining Hardware | Avoid implying grids exist for mining. |
| Electricity | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | High | Electricity is the carrier that makes modern computation and mining practical. | ASICs; Electric infrastructure examples | Do not reduce energy history to electricity. |
| Steam Power | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | Medium | Steam gives a genuine industrial-energy predecessor. | Industrial examples in Deeper | Do not revive Industrial Power as a vague catch-all node. |
| Water and Wind Power | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Earlier mechanical power shows energy systems before industrial heat engines. | Mills; maritime movement in Deeper | Avoid a simple ladder from water to steam to Bitcoin. |
| Animal and Human Work | provisional | Period | Deep human history | Medium | Work before machines gives a historical baseline for labour and power. | Labour examples in Deeper | Do not moralise work as proof-of-work ancestry. |
| Fire and Biological Energy | provisional | Period | Deep human history | Medium | Fire and food energy anchor the oldest energy transformations. | Combustion and metabolism examples | Do not force exact origin dates. |

### Cryptography Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Digital Signatures | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Signatures prove authorisation in Bitcoin. | Private Keys; Address Derivation | Do not describe signatures as encryption. |
| Hash Functions | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Hashing links data, commitments and proof-of-work puzzles. | Merkle Trees; Hashcash; Proof of Work | Do not collapse hashing into secrecy. |
| Public-Key Cryptography | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Public-key systems explain proof without shared secrets. | Digital Signatures; Private Keys | Avoid implying public-key systems alone create money. |
| Computer-Era Cryptanalysis | provisional | Period | Networked and computing era | Medium | Modern cryptography grew with attacks, analysis and standards. | Security assumptions in Deeper | Do not make cryptanalysis a Bitcoin ancestor by itself. |
| Mechanical Encryption | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | Medium | Mechanical systems bridge classical ciphers and computer-era cryptography. | Encryption as Deeper context | Encryption is not central to Bitcoin's base protocol. |
| Classical Ciphers | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Older secrecy practices anchor the pre-computer history. | Historical cipher examples | Do not present secrecy as the main Bitcoin mechanism. |

### Computation Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Distributed Computing | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Bitcoin is executed and checked across many machines. | Consensus Protocols; Validation | Do not imply Bitcoin invented distributed computation. |
| Digital Computers | provisional | Period | Networked and computing era | High | Digital computers make protocol execution ordinary. | Transistors; Data Structures | Avoid a hardware-only history. |
| Transistors | provisional | Period | Networked and computing era | High | Transistors anchor modern computation materially. | ASICs; Digital Computers | Do not overfit hardware to Bitcoin mining. |
| Programmable Machines | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | Medium | Programmability explains rule-following machines before modern software. | Turing Machines | Avoid a single invention story. |
| Turing Machines | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Turing machines clarify computation as formal rule execution. | Algorithms as Deeper context | Algorithms is too broad for a structural bubble here. |
| Boolean Logic | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Boolean logic connects formal reasoning to digital circuits. | Transistors; validation logic | Do not turn logic into a chronology. |
| Mechanical Calculation | provisional | Period | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Mechanical calculation provides a pre-digital calculating-machine anchor. | Calculation devices in Deeper | Do not imply direct descent to Bitcoin validation. |
| Counting and Record-Keeping | provisional | Period | Deep human history | Contested | Counting and records connect computation to older social memory. | Ledgers; Social Accounting | Do not duplicate the Money route. |

### Networks Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Peer-to-Peer Networks | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Bitcoin's relay and node topology belong here. | Full Nodes; Gossip Propagation; Peer Discovery | Do not equate peer-to-peer with automatic decentralisation. |
| Internet Protocols | provisional | Persistent | Networked and computing era | High | Bitcoin depends on internet communication layers. | Routing; Censorship Resistance | Do not make "the internet" a single bubble. |
| Packet Switching | provisional | Period | Networked and computing era | High | Packet switching is a hinge behind internet communication. | Routing | Avoid treating it as identical to Bitcoin relay. |
| Telecommunications | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | Medium | Electrical communication predates packet networks. | Telegraphy | Keep distinct from payment networks. |
| Telegraphy | provisional | Period | Electrical and industrial era | High | Telegraphy is the historical hinge for long-distance electrical messaging. | Message routing examples | Do not reduce it to one device or inventor. |
| Postal and Messenger Networks | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Pre-electrical networks show organised long-distance message movement. | Institutional relay examples | Avoid implying all networks decentralise over time. |
| Writing and Signalling | provisional | Period | Deep human history | Contested | Durable signs and signals predate formal communications networks. | Record and signal examples | Do not invent exact origin dates. |

### Government & Power Spine

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Modern Regulatory Systems | provisional | Period | Bitcoin and contemporary digital era | High | Contemporary rules shape access to banking, exchanges and settlement. | Banking Regulation; Capital Controls; Sanctions | Do not collapse regulation into opposition to Bitcoin. |
| Monetary Sovereignty | provisional | Persistent | Early modern institutional era | Medium | State authority over units, taxes and payment law differs from the Money strand's Political Control node. | Legal Tender; Central Banks | Avoid duplicate use as a vague synonym for power. |
| Fiscal States | provisional | Period | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Fiscal states connect taxation, debt and administrative capacity. | Public Debt; Taxation | Do not imply one European pathway is universal. |
| Public Debt | provisional | Period | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Public debt explains credit, war finance and fiscal credibility. | War Finance | Keep distinct from private credit. |
| War Finance | provisional | Recurring | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Contested | War repeatedly pressures money, debt and taxation. | Public Debt; Seigniorage | Avoid treating war as the only driver of monetary change. |
| Organised Coercion | provisional | Recurring | Deep human history | Contested | Armies and enforcement explain power behind tribute, law and taxation. | State Capacity; Property Enforcement | Avoid flattening states into pure coercion. |
| State Capacity | provisional | Persistent | Early modern institutional era | Medium | Capacity explains the ability to tax, enforce and administer systems. | Modern Regulatory Systems | Keep separate from legitimacy claims. |
| Taxation | provisional | Recurring | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Tax obligations help explain units, payment law and state money. | Legal Tender; State Coinage | Do not claim taxes alone create money. |
| State Coinage | provisional | Period | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Coinage shows authority marking and standardising money objects. | Mints; Seigniorage | Keep distinct from all coinage. |
| Tribute | provisional | Recurring | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Medium | Tribute gives an older route into obligation, power and extraction. | Taxation; Law | Do not make tribute and taxation identical. |
| Law | provisional | Recurring | Medieval and ancient recorded history | Contested | Law frames enforceable obligations, property and settlement. | Property Rights; Legal Tender | Avoid one legal tradition standing for all. |

### Philosophy & Time Genealogy

This strand uses an intellectual genealogy rather than strict chronology.

| Spine node | Review status | Temporal kind | Broad era | Time confidence | Why it belongs on the spine | Branches attached | Risks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bitcoin Rules | provisional | Period | Bitcoin and contemporary digital era | High | Protocol rules provide the concrete point of departure. | Rules and Discretion; Validation | Do not turn "rules" into "code settles every question". |
| Verification | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Verification explains checking rather than trusting a claim. | Full Nodes; Trust | Do not imply all trust disappears. |
| Trust | provisional | Recurring | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Trust is reduced, shifted or formalised across routes. | Social Memory; Legal Tender as preview-only | Avoid treating trust as a single substance. |
| Property Rights | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Property explains claims of control, exclusion and transfer. | Custody; Ownership | Do not make property theory a Bitcoin proof. |
| Ownership | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Ownership narrows property into recognised control claims. | Possession as Deeper context | Do not retain Possession as a separate structural bubble. |
| Value | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Value anchors the difference between price, utility and judgement. | Subjective Valuation; Exchange | Avoid making value a promotional claim. |
| Exchange | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Exchange connects valuation to coordination between people. | Money Functions | Do not make exchange equal monetary exchange only. |
| Saving | provisional | Recurring | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Contested | Saving links value across time without duplicating Store of Value. | Time Preference; Purchasing Power | Avoid treating saving as endorsement of one monetary theory. |
| Time Preference | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Time preference explains present and future valuation. | Saving | Keep contested economic interpretations visible. |
| Common Knowledge | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Common knowledge explains coordination around shared expectations. | Shared Truth; Schelling Points in Deeper | Do not keep Schelling Points as a structural bubble yet. |
| Shared Truth | provisional | Atemporal | Atemporal / recurring concepts | Not applicable | Shared truth frames agreement about records and rules. | Finality; Verification | Do not over-philosophise technical finality. |

## Approved Shared Candidates

Target: approximately 15-20 approved shared candidates. This revision approves 18.

| Canonical node | Review status | Intended route contexts | Why approved |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Settlement | provisional | Money; Networks; Government & Power | Payment finality, rail design and legal settlement all need the same canonical concept. |
| Custody | provisional | Money; Cryptography; Philosophy & Time | Control over assets links institutions, keys and ownership claims. |
| Trust | provisional | Money; Cryptography; Philosophy & Time | Trust is shifted between people, institutions, verification and rules. |
| Ledgers | provisional | Money; Computation; Philosophy & Time | Records of obligation, state and transaction history belong across routes. |
| Blockchain | provisional | Money; Computation; Cryptography; Networks | Blockchain joins ledger structure, hash commitments, replication and propagation. |
| Central Banks | provisional | Money; Government & Power; Networks | Central banks connect fiat systems, reserves and settlement infrastructure. |
| Legal Tender | provisional | Money; Government & Power | Legal settlement rules must stay narrow and source-led. |
| Proof of Work | provisional | Energy; Cryptography; Computation | Proof of work is energy expenditure, a hash puzzle and a coordination mechanism. |
| Hash Functions | provisional | Cryptography; Energy; Computation | Hashes are commitments and proof-of-work inputs. |
| Digital Signatures | provisional | Cryptography; Money | Signatures connect spend authority with public verification. |
| Private Keys | provisional | Cryptography; Money; Philosophy & Time | Private keys connect secret control, custody and ownership risk. |
| Peer-to-Peer Networks | provisional | Networks; Computation | Peer relay and distributed system design share the same architecture concept. |
| Full Nodes | provisional | Computation; Networks; Trust | Full nodes check rules and participate in relay. |
| Property Rights | provisional | Philosophy & Time; Government & Power; Money | Ownership claims require legal, philosophical and custody framing. |
| Finality | provisional | Philosophy & Time; Money; Computation | Finality links settlement confidence, time and ledger reorganisation risk. |
| Monetary Sovereignty | provisional | Government & Power; Money | State authority over money needs both political and monetary framing. |
| Public-Key Cryptography | provisional | Cryptography; Money | Public-key proof explains non-custodial control without central accounts. |
| Digital Money | provisional | Money; Cryptography; Networks | Digital money links monetary function, digital cash experiments and networked payments. |

## Preview-Only Also Touches Register

These 17 relationships are informational previews only. They should not become navigable edges without a new proposal and human review.

| Relationship | Review status | Canonical status | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Energy also touches Scarcity | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Scarcity is not an energy-history parent. |
| Gold also touches Costly Production | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Extraction cost is relevant but does not make Gold a proof-of-work ancestor. |
| Privacy also touches Censorship Resistance | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | The relation is real but too broad for a navigable parent edge. |
| Legal Tender also touches Paper Claims | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Legal enforceability and paper promises overlap but should stay distinct. |
| Trust also touches Legal Tender | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Legal tender may affect trust without becoming a Trust child. |
| Digital Payments also touches Digital Cash | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Digital cash is a historical branch; digital payments is a broader system. |
| Payment Networks also touches Banking Rails | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Payment networks are broader than bank account rails. |
| Payment Networks also touches Central Bank Settlement | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Settlement infrastructure is related, not the whole network. |
| Peer-to-Peer Networks also touches Censorship Resistance | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Topology can help resistance but does not guarantee it. |
| Open-Source Software also touches Political Control | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Public code can affect governance without becoming a state-power node. |
| Software Forks also touches Rules and Discretion | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Forks expose discretion around rules but are not a philosophy parent. |
| Full Nodes also touches Trust | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Verification changes trust assumptions without eliminating trust. |
| Property Rights also touches Custody | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Legal ownership and practical control must remain distinct. |
| Finality also touches Settlement | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Settlement is one important form of finality. |
| Monetary Sovereignty also touches Capital Controls | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Capital controls are a policy branch, not the definition. |
| Monetary Sovereignty also touches Sanctions | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | Sanctions are a policy branch, not the definition. |
| War Finance also touches Seigniorage | provisional | canonical source; canonical target | War finance can pressure issuance and extraction without making seigniorage its parent. |

## Revision Decision Log

### Specified Weak Candidates

| Candidate | Decision | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stranded Energy | Move to Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining or Energy Markets | Too source-sensitive and claim-shaped for a structural bubble. |
| Demand Response | Move to Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining | Useful energy-market example, not a core atlas node. |
| Thermodynamic Cost | Merge into Costly Production | The phrase is too abstract and risks duplicating proof-of-work cost language. |
| Industrial Power | Merge into Steam Power, Electricity and Electrical Grids and Computation | Too broad as a node; the historical spine now uses clearer anchors. |
| Encryption | Move to Deeper under Mechanical Encryption, Classical Ciphers or Privacy | Encryption is not central to Bitcoin's base protocol. |
| Version Control | Move to Deeper under Open-Source Software | Relevant to software practice, but weak as a structural Bitcoin route. |
| Liquidity Networks | Merge into Payment Networks or Lightning payment-channel discussion | Duplicates liquidity and network effects without a clear canonical concept. |
| Tor | Convert to preview-only example under Privacy and Censorship Resistance | Important tool, but too specific for current depth-four structure. |
| Deferred Consumption | Merge into Saving and Time Preference | The concept repeats the time trade-off already covered there. |
| Schelling Points | Move to Deeper under Common Knowledge | Useful example, but too narrow for the first philosophy structure. |
| Card Networks | Move to Deeper under Payment Networks | Concrete example of payment infrastructure, not a necessary node. |
| Bank Transfers | Merge into Banking Rails | Account transfer mechanics belong inside the broader banking-rails node. |
| Possession | Merge into Ownership and Property Rights | Physical control should be handled as a subtopic, not a separate bubble. |

### Duplicate And Category Decisions

| Issue | Decision | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Nodes / Full Nodes | Use Full Nodes | "Nodes" is too broad; Full Nodes is the Bitcoin-specific canonical concept. |
| Payment Networks / Banking Rails | Keep both with scoped meanings | Payment Networks is the broader network concept; Banking Rails is bank-account infrastructure. |
| Bank Transfers / Banking Rails | Merge into Banking Rails | Transfers are an example inside the rails system. |
| Finality / Settlement Finality / Irreversibility | Keep Finality as canonical | Settlement finality and irreversibility become contextual explanations. |
| Political Control / Monetary Sovereignty | Keep distinct | Political Control is the Money-route framing; Monetary Sovereignty is state authority over units, tax and payment law. |
| Energy / Thermodynamic Cost / Costly Production | Keep Energy and Costly Production; merge Thermodynamic Cost | Costly Production is the reusable mechanism; Thermodynamic Cost is wording, not a node. |
| Store of Value / Saving / Purchasing Power | Keep all three narrowly scoped | Store of Value is a money function; Saving is a practice; Purchasing Power is an outcome measure. |
| Gold Mining / Gold / Costly Production | Do not keep Gold Mining | Gold Mining belongs in Deeper under Gold or Costly Production. |
| Lender of Last Resort | Category corrected to Practice | It is a central-bank function/practice, not an institution. |
| Gold -> Commodity Money | Edge corrected | Commodity Money branches from Monetary Metals, not from Gold. |
| Inflation and Debasement | Parent corrected | These are not one simple consequence of Seigniorage; they need separate historical treatment. |

### Historical Gaps Filled

| Strand | Historical nodes added | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Money | Digital Money; Modern Fiat and Credit Systems; Coinage; Monetary Metals; Social Accounting; Banking; Specialisation | Repairs the money spine without reviving barter as a universal origin. |
| Energy | Electrical Grids and Computation; Water and Wind Power; Animal and Human Work; Fire and Biological Energy | Replaces a mining-heavy route with genuine energy history. |
| Networks | Internet Protocols; Telecommunications; Telegraphy; Postal and Messenger Networks; Writing and Signalling | Adds pre-internet communications history and treats Telegraphy as a hinge. |
| Computation | Distributed Computing; Digital Computers; Transistors; Programmable Machines; Turing Machines; Boolean Logic; Counting and Record-Keeping | Adds a rule-execution and machine-history spine. |
| Cryptography | Computer-Era Cryptanalysis; Mechanical Encryption | Separates secrecy history from Bitcoin's signature and hash mechanisms. |
| Government & Power | Modern Regulatory Systems; Fiscal States; Public Debt; War Finance; Organised Coercion; State Capacity; Tribute; Law | Gives the strand its own state-power history instead of duplicating Money's Political Control branch. |
| Philosophy & Time | Bitcoin Rules; Verification; Value; Exchange; Subjective Valuation; Time; Rules and Discretion; Shared Truth | Converts the strand into an intellectual genealogy with concrete review targets. |

### Relationships Downgraded To Also Touches

| Relationship | Decision | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Energy -> Scarcity | Downgrade | Energy may illuminate scarcity claims but should not host Scarcity navigably. |
| Trust -> Legal Tender | Downgrade | Legal force can affect trust without becoming a Trust child. |
| Privacy -> Censorship Resistance | Downgrade | The connection is real but too broad and politically loaded for a first navigable edge. |
| Government & Power -> Energy Markets | Downgrade | Energy markets are regulated, but they belong to Energy. |
| Legal Tender -> Paper Claims | Downgrade | Both involve enforceability and claims, but neither contains the other. |

### Deferred Non-Canonical Examples

| Example | Decision | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Energy Markets | Keep out of Also touches until canonical status is approved | The term is relevant to mining and regulation but is not currently a canonical node in this plan. |
| Demand Response | Use only as a Deeper example under Bitcoin Mining or future Energy Markets material | Demand response is an energy-market example, not a canonical atlas node for this implementation pass. |
| Stranded Energy | Use only as a source-sensitive Deeper example | Stranded energy is claim-shaped and should not appear as a preview endpoint without a canonical proposal. |

### Shared-Node Accounting Note

The approved shared-candidate list is declared separately from realised multi-parent nodes and multi-strand placements.

## Future Implementation Batches

These batches do not implement anything. Each batch still requires bubble proposals, source inventories, human approval, validation, manual editorial review and a commit.

| Batch | Gate status | Blockers | Explicit blocker summary | Nodes | Why this order |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. Existing Money sourcing backlog | blocked-pending-human-review | 50 | source-gaps: Settlement; Custody; Ledgers; Trust; Blockchain; Central Banks; Legal Tender; source-review: Bitcoin as Money; Scarcity; trust-edge-review: Bitcoin as Money -> Trust; Philosophy & Time -> Trust; settlement-scope-review: Settlement; shared-context-review: Settlement; Trust; Blockchain; Central Banks; Legal Tender; source-backlog: Batch 1 | Bitcoin as Money; Scarcity; Settlement; Custody; Ledgers; Trust; Blockchain; Central Banks; Legal Tender | Cleans up existing public Money nodes before adding new structure. |
| 2. Core shared Bitcoin mechanisms | blocked-pending-human-review | 31 |  | Proof of Work; Hash Functions; Digital Signatures; Private Keys; Full Nodes; Validation; UTXO Model; Difficulty Adjustment | Establishes the shared technical concepts needed by later strands. |
| 3. Historical Networks spine | blocked-pending-human-review | 22 |  | Peer-to-Peer Networks; Internet Protocols; Packet Switching; Telecommunications; Telegraphy; Postal and Messenger Networks; Writing and Signalling | Adds communications history before network mechanisms sprawl. |
| 4. Historical Computation spine | blocked-pending-human-review | 24 |  | Distributed Computing; Digital Computers; Transistors; Programmable Machines; Turing Machines; Boolean Logic; Mechanical Calculation; Counting and Record-Keeping | Builds rule-execution and machine history before adding detailed computation branches. |
| 5. Government & Power spine | blocked-pending-human-review | 28 |  | Modern Regulatory Systems; Monetary Sovereignty; Fiscal States; Public Debt; War Finance; State Capacity; Taxation; State Coinage; Law | Gives state power its own spine, separate from Money's Political Control framing. |
| 6. Energy history spine | blocked-pending-human-review | 21 |  | Bitcoin Mining; Electrical Grids and Computation; Electricity; Steam Power; Water and Wind Power; Animal and Human Work; Fire and Biological Energy | Replaces the mining-heavy energy route with real energy history. |

## Unresolved Editorial Questions

- Should Scarcity remain a Money-only navigable node with Energy as preview-only, or does it need a carefully scoped shared parent later?
- Should Digital Money be a shared canonical node, or should Digital Cash carry most of the cryptography lineage?
- Does Modern Fiat and Credit Systems need splitting before implementation?
- Is Social Accounting too broad, or is it the right non-barter historical anchor?
- Should Monetary Sovereignty be implemented before or after the existing Political Control copy is reviewed?
- Should Philosophy & Time keep Time as a node, or only use it as metadata and Deeper context?
- Which historical regions should anchor Coinage, Taxation, Tribute and Law without implying universal development?

## Definition Of Planning Done

This map is ready for human pruning when:

- the seven historical spines have been approved or amended
- duplicate and merge decisions have been reviewed
- approved shared candidates have approved parent rationales where they are navigable
- Also touches relationships are confirmed as preview-only
- temporal metadata has been copied into each bubble proposal
- source categories have been converted into real source inventories
- the first implementation batch has approved proposals
- no route is kept merely to reach depth four
- semantic validator warnings have been reviewed or resolved
- Also touches endpoints are canonical nodes only
- approved shared candidates, realised multi-parent nodes and multi-strand placements are reported separately
- each implementation batch has an explicit gate status
- batch-gated validation passes before any public implementation begins
